summary: In this 1997 internal presentation, Steve Jobs outlines his strategy for revitalizing Apple shortly after his return. He emphasizes simplifying the product line, drastically reducing inventory, and returning to Apple's core values through the iconic 'Think Different' marketing campaign.

principles.rationale: In a noisy and complicated world, a company only gets a small chance to be remembered, so it must be crystal clear about what it stands for rather than just listing product features.
principles.source_quote: to me marketing is about values this is a very complicated world it's a very noisy world and we're not going to get a chance to get people to remember much about us

principles.statement: People with passion can change the world for the better.
principles.rationale: This is the core belief that drives Apple's existence and connects it to its customers and developers who push the human race forward.
principles.source_quote: Apple at the core its core value is that we believe that people with passion can change the world for the better

principles.statement: Core values must remain constant even as everything else changes.
principles.rationale: While markets, products, distribution, and manufacturing strategies must evolve over time, the fundamental beliefs of a company should never change.
principles.source_quote: values and core values those things shouldn't change the things that Apple believed in at its core are the same things that Apple really stands for today

principles.statement: Even the greatest brands require investment and care.
principles.rationale: Without active maintenance and investment, even top-tier global brands will lose their relevance and vitality over time.
principles.source_quote: even a great brand needs invest investment and caring if it's going to retain its relevance and vitality

mental_models.name: The Nike Model of Marketing
mental_models.description: A marketing lens where a brand selling a commodity elevates itself by honoring the greatness of its users rather than discussing product features.
mental_models.example: Nike never talks about 'air soles' or why they are better than Reebok; they honor great athletes. Apple applies this by honoring the 'crazy ones' who change the world, rather than talking about megahertz or Windows.

heuristics: Get back to the basics of great products, great marketing, and great distribution.
Cut the product roadmap down to the 30% that are gems.
Take inventory out of the pipeline so the customer can tell you what they want.

signature_quotes.text: to me marketing is about values
signature_quotes.context: Explaining why Apple's new ad campaign won't focus on computer specifications.

signature_quotes.text: those people that are crazy enough to think they can change the world are the ones that actually do
signature_quotes.context: Describing the core belief behind the 'Think Different' campaign.

signature_quotes.text: I think the question now is is is is not can we turn around Apple I think that's the booby prize I think it's can we make apple really great again
signature_quotes.context: Closing remarks to the Apple staff regarding the company's future.

anti_patterns.description: Marketing based on 'speeds and feeds' or comparing features to competitors.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It fails to communicate the brand's core identity in a noisy world. Customers don't connect with technical specs like megahertz; they connect with values.

anti_patterns.description: Guessing customer demand months in advance and building massive inventory pipelines.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: No one is smart enough to accurately predict demand 4-6 months out. It leads to bloat and inefficiency instead of responding to actual customer demand super fast.

anti_patterns.description: Maintaining a bloated, complex product line.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It creates a lack of focus internally and confuses the customer to the point where they can't figure out what to buy.
